source_jobs.title: Director of Business Development
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Director of Business Development as part of our Growth Team. As Director of Business Development you will drive Gallatin's revenue engine and fuel our transition from early Army and Air Force wins into scaled adoption across the full Joint Logistics Enterprise, DLA, broader federal agencies (DHS, FEMA, DOS, Intel Community, DOI), state-level programs, commercial B2B logistics ecosystems, and — longer term — international defense partners. Reporting directly to the Head of Growth, you will lead end-to-end business development, capture execution, and sales for Navigator, translating its predictive sustainment capabilities into funded programs, program-of-record transitions, and dual-use commercial wins.

This is a critical role built for a mission-focused tech sales leader — someone who has scaled public-sector AI or defense tech revenue 200%+ YoY, thrives in ambiguity, builds high-velocity pipelines in complex acquisitions, and closes transformative deals. You will shape GTM strategy, hunt and close major opportunities, and expand our footprint from current DoW/service focus into new federal, commercial, and future international markets — all while collaborating closely with our senior capture manager and the broader growth team.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Public Sector & Commercial Revenue Growth

 - Own the full business development and sales lifecycle for multi-million to nine-figure opportunities with DoW customers (Army, DLA, Joint Staff, Air Force, USMC, Combatant Commands) while rapidly expanding into other federal agencies (DHS, FEMA for HADR/logistics optimization, DOS, Intel Community, DOI), state-level programs, and B2B commercial logistics partners.

 - Build and execute a high-velocity pipeline targeting contested logistics priorities (predictive demand forecasting, resupply optimization, supply chain resilience) and dual-use applications (disaster response, global supply chain, critical infrastructure), leveraging vehicles like SBIR/STTR, OTAs, Tradewinds, FAR/DFARS, and commercial pathways.

 - Deliver aggressive revenue targets by converting early Army/Air Force footholds into broader Joint, interagency, commercial, and (longer-term) allied international adoption.

Prime & Integrator Opportunity Development

 - Identify and secure key opportunities with primes, systems integrators, commercial logistics leaders, and technology collaborators (AI/ML, sensing, autonomy providers) to accelerate market access, co-selling, and solution delivery.

 - Cultivate and maintain senior-level relationships across OSW, DLA, Service logistics commands, other federal agencies, and commercial stakeholders to shape requirements and secure long-term program funding and commercial contracts.

Team Building & Operational Excellence

 - Recruit, mentor, and lead a high-performing BD/sales team (account executives, solutions consultants) while partnering closely with our existing senior capture manager to drive scalable processes for pipeline management, proposal execution, and win-rate optimization.

 - Partner cross-functionally with product, engineering, and customer success to ensure seamless delivery and rapid iteration based on warfighter, agency, and commercial customer feedback.

 - Provide executive-level reporting on pipeline health, competitive intelligence, and strategic risks to inform company roadmaps and growth strategy.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Active Top Secret clearance (SCI preferred) or clear eligibility and willingness to obtain

 - 8–12+ years of progressive business development and sales leadership in federal technology or

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Senior Solutions Architect to lead the design, deployment, and operation of agentic AI platforms in high-stakes, mission-critical government environments. This is a forward-deployed, customer-facing role that sits at the intersection of modern AI technology and national security — ideal for someone who has moved fluidly between the operational military world and the commercial defense tech industry.

You’ll serve as the primary technical authority with customers and program offices, translating complex mission requirements into scalable, secure architectures — and then rolling up your sleeves to make sure they get built and delivered. You’re equally comfortable in a briefing room with senior government stakeholders as you are in a terminal troubleshooting a production system.



WHAT YOU'LL DO
SOLUTION ARCHITECTURE

 - Conceptualize and design end-to-end technical solutions for customer-facing agentic AI and data platforms tailored to government mission requirements, including classified and austere network environments.

Customer Engagement

 - Serve as the senior technical point of contact for program offices and mission partners, building credibility and trust through deep technical competence and operational understanding. Map the end-user experience to our software and hardware solutions and be the critical technical translator between customer and our engineering & product teams.

Forward Deployment

 - Work directly at or alongside customer sites to deploy, operate, and continuously improve AI/ML enabled software platforms — owning outcomes from initial design through production.

Cross-Functional Leadership

 - Partner with product, engineering, and business development teams to shape roadmaps, respond to RFPs, and ensure delivered solutions align with both mission needs and company strategy.

Compliance & Security

 - Navigate and drive security architecture requirements including FedRAMP, IL4/IL5/IL6, Zero Trust frameworks, and RMF/ATO processes.

Automate & Optimize

 - Identify opportunities to reduce manual operations, automate workflows, and improve reliability and scalability of deployed systems to maximize customer experience.
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Active security clearance preferred; TS/SCI a plus.

 - 10+ years of experience spanning software/systems engineering, infrastructure, or technical program leadership — ideally with a mix of military, government, and commercial defense tech.

 - Hands-on experience deploying and operating platforms in classified or government cloud environments (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government, IL4/IL5/IL6).

 - Familiarity with modern AI/ML technologies including LLMs and agentic AI systems, and a passion for applying them to real mission problems.

 - Proven ability to engage senior government stakeholders — translating technical concepts into mission value and building long-term relationships.

 - Strong working knowledge of Kubernetes, cloud infrastructure, and modern DevSecOps practices.

 - Proficiency in Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, or similar languages.

 - Experience navigating compliance frameworks: FedRAMP, RMF/ATO, Zero Trust, NIST.

 - Comfort operating in ambiguous, fast-moving environments with high stakes and limited playbooks. This position is hands-on and embedded with customers.



BONUS POINTS

 - Military service with a technical or special programs background.

 - Experience at defense tech companies operating at the civil/military AI frontier.

 - Prior experience bridging product, engineering, and government customers.

 - Familiarity with JADC2, C2 sys

source_jobs.title: Technical Product Manager, Navigator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Technical Product Manager to own the technical side of how Navigator gets built, from the architecture conversation through the clean handoff to engineering. Navigator turns tactical-to-strategic sustainment data into decisions, and it only works when product intent and technical reality stay aligned across partner integrations and government systems of record.

You will sit between product and engineering and make sure both halves stay tightly aligned on what we are building and how. This is a hands-on-the-architecture role: you keep specs honest against the system, surface technical constraints early, and prevent the rebuilds that come from calls made too soon. Your focus is the integrity of the build and the handoff, partnering closely with engineers on the how and with product on the what, so that nothing gets lost in the gap between what we decided and what shipped.



WHAT YOU'LL DO


SHAPE TECHNICAL PRODUCT STRATEGY

 - Guide product decisions with architectural insight, so we commit to designs the system can actually support.

 - Partner with product and design early, turning “what if” into “here is how” before a sprint is on the line.

 - Champion technical excellence while keeping the team fast.


BRIDGE DOMAINS

 - Be the technical voice in product debates and the product voice in technical ones.

 - Translate Navigator’s integration surface, including partner systems like Anduril Lattice and Palantir Foundry and government systems of record like Maven Smart System, GCSS-Army, Vantage, and ADVANA, into requirements engineering can build against.

 - Run the pre-development sessions where product intent meets technical constraint and drive them to a real decision.


SHIP PRODUCTS THAT MATTER

 - Own the technical side of the lifecycle from concept through deployment, with clean handoffs and clear success criteria.

 - Write specs that hold up against the architecture and do not drift mid-build.

 - Jump into sprint planning and stand-ups to unblock the team and make real-time trade-offs.



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3 to 5 years of software development experience, with a focus on systems thinking and architecture.

 - Proven ability to dive deep into technical specs and explain complex concepts to non-technical partners.

 - Experience with APIs, distributed systems, and modern development practices.

 - A track record working closely with both product and engineering, holding the line between technical excellence and shipping value.

 - Comfort with agile methodologies and modern collaboration tools.

 - Strong written and verbal communication, especially translating between domains.

 - U.S. citizenship with the ability to obtain a security clearance.




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience integrating with enterprise or government systems of record, or with partner platforms in a multi-vendor environment.

 - Data platform experience; Advana or Palantir Foundry knowledge is a strong plus.

 - Exposure to defense, logistics, or mission-critical B2B systems.

 - A knack for making complex systems feel simple to users.



MISSION AND IDENTITY

We are building the system that enables faster, smarter logistics decisions in contested environments, and we're doing it with a team of seasoned entrepreneurs, operators, and technologists who have built and scaled solutions in this space before. We hold ourselves to an extremely high standard. We value clear thinking, direct communication, and the kind of ownership that doesn't stop until something actually works.

Our mission is to create decision advantage when the stakes are the highe

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Navigator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own the theater tier of Navigator, our core sustainment decision support product, at the level where COCOM and MAJCOM staffs plan and run logistics. This is the strategic expression of Navigator: the same platform that supports tactical and operational sustainment, extended to the echelon where theater-wide priorities, constraints, and trade-offs get decided.

You will own the roadmap for this tier end to end, from discovery with theater logistics planners through release and real adoption inside operational commands. You will work directly with COCOM and MAJCOM J4 and A4 staffs, program offices, engineering, design, and our leadership team, and you will be accountable for whether what we ship improves how theater sustainment decisions get made.

This role demands business acumen and fluency in the customer. You should understand how the Department of War is organized, how it plans, how it buys, and what it takes to build software that gets fielded and used inside it. We are building for operators in the most demanding environments on earth, and the person in this seat translates that reality into product.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

The Navigator Theater Roadmap

 - Set priorities for the theater tier, make the trade-offs, and defend them to engineering, leadership, and the customer in a way that earns trust.

 - Decide what we build and, just as importantly, what we say no to, keeping the tier focused on the decisions that matter most at COCOM and MAJCOM echelon.

 - Turn an ambitious multi-echelon vision into a sequenced set of bets with clear success metrics.

Discovery With the People Who Plan at Theater

 - Run discovery directly with COCOM and MAJCOM logistics planners, J4 and Service-4 staff, and program offices, in garrison and in the field.

 - Write the synthesis that turns what you hear into product direction, not feature requests, and tie each decision to a mission outcome.

 - Stay close enough to the work that you can tell the difference between what users ask for and what they need.

Execution End to End

 - Own the product lifecycle from problem statement through release and adoption, working shoulder to shoulder with engineering and design through sprint planning, demos, and field feedback.

 - Know whether what shipped moved the mission and be honest when it didn't.

Be the Voice of the Customer

 - Bring deep understanding of how the Department of War plans, buys, and fields software into every product decision.

 - Make sure what we build fits how theater commands operate and shorten the distance between a good idea and a fielded capability.



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 7+ years in product, with ownership of complex enterprise, defense, logistics, data, or AI products.

 - A track record of shipping major capabilities from discovery through adoption, not just launch.

 - Proficiency using AI in your own work process, and a track record of shipping products that build AI into the capability they deliver.

 - Deep understanding of the Department of War: how it is organized, how it plans, how it buys, and what it takes to build and field software that gets used inside it.

 - Sharp written communication: product briefs, trade-off memos, and narratives that hold up under scrutiny.

 - Comfort working directly with senior stakeholders and end users, earning trust quickly with both.

 - Bias toward action in an environment where priorities shift as new mission and technical context emerges.

 - U.S. citizenship; ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance.




BONUS POINTS

 - Background in s

source_jobs.title: Proposal & Capture Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Proposal & Capture Manager within our Go-To-Market Team to scale Gallatin’s impact across the United States Government. Reporting to the Capture Lead, you will partner directly with the proposal & capture function and the broader Go-To-Market team to double the volume of capture and proposal work Gallatin can run at any given time. You will own end-to-end execution on assigned solicitations — from compliance matrix to color reviews to on-time submission — while supporting capture activities that shape the win before the RFP drops.

As a Proposal & Capture Manager you are passionate, knowledgeable, and curious about artificial intelligence and military logistics. You have a high attention to detail and are an excellent planner. You will own the day-to-day execution of assigned proposals, collaborating cross-functionally to bring the best of Gallatin to our customers in written communication. You should feel comfortable working towards tight deadlines and thrive in ambiguous environments.

You have a bias toward finding not just one-off solutions, but repeatable and scalable ways to ensure we continually improve our process. You have a track record of managing winning submissions across DoD/DoW and civilian agencies (DHS, DOJ, HHS, VA, USDA, and others), ranging from multi-volume RFP responses through quad chart submissions.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Collaborate with the Capture Lead, Head of Business Development, executive stakeholders, and internal subject matter experts to assess Gallatin’s alignment to solicitation requirements

 - Develop and execute detailed proposal plans to ensure Gallatin submits timely and compelling responses across DoD/DoW and civilian agency opportunities

 - Write, edit, and design compelling materials for submission — narrative volumes, past performance, quad charts, white papers, and management volumes

 - Run color-team reviews (Pink, Red, Gold) with discipline and drive revisions to a higher-quality final deliverable

 - Support capture activities for priority pursuits, including research, competitive analysis, customer call plans, teaming strategy, and win-theme development

 - Collaborate with teaming partners to develop wholistic solutions

 - Maintain Gallatin’s proposal library — boilerplate, past performance, resumes, win themes, graphics, and templates — and keep it current as the company grows

 - Establish a cycle of continuous improvement for Gallatin’s capture and proposal development process



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Minimum 5 years of work experience in federal proposal and capture management across DoD/DoW and/or civilian agencies

 - A technical background (education or professional experience with STEM) and track record of working with advanced technology solutions

 - Working fluency with the federal acquisition environment — FAR-based contracting, OTAs, IDIQ task orders, SBIR/STTR, and sources-sought / RFI processes

 - Advanced cross-functional experience and collaborative ability

 - Expert verbal and written communications

 - A track record of going zero to one in a role

 - Ability to operate independently and lead indirectly

 - Must be eligible to obtain and maintain a U.S. government security clearance



BONUS POINTS

 - Active U.S. government security clearance

 - Prior experience in defense artificial intelligence solutions

 - Prior experience in logistics or sustainment solutions

 - Prior proposal or capture experience inside a defense-tech startup or growth-stage federal contractor

 - Shipley APMP certification or equivalent formal training



MISSION AND IDENTITY

source_jobs.title: Mission Development Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

A Mission Development Manager at Gallatin is responsible for identifying, shaping, and advancing mission-aligned growth opportunities across the federal defense, national security, and adjacent commercial markets. The role sits at the intersection of customer discovery, operational problem framing, solution positioning, capture support, and partner coordination.

This role requires understanding how military and government customers define mission problems, how acquisition pathways mature, how operational requirements translate into funded opportunities, and how Gallatin’s Navigator platform can be positioned against priority workflows in sustainment, logistics, mobility, readiness, and mission planning. Reporting to the Head of Business Development, you will own a defined portfolio — building qualified pipeline, developing customer and partner relationships, shaping early opportunities, supporting capture activities, and ensuring Gallatin’s external growth motion stays grounded in real mission needs.

We are hiring against multiple portfolios now and expect to open additional Mission Development Manager seats as the year progresses. Portfolios are aligned to the markets where Gallatin can move the most logistics readiness in the shortest time:

 - Army / SOCOM / Navy — service-level commands, program executive offices, operational units, and the formations that move and sustain the force.

 - DLA / GSA / TRANSCOM / AMC — the joint and enterprise sustainment, mobility, and acquisition organizations that run U.S. logistics at scale.

 - Federal civilian (non-DoD) — State Department, DHS (including FEMA), DOE, and adjacent civilian agencies whose missions hinge on resilient logistics.

 - Commercial — large logistics-heavy industry and the technology enablers that move freight, fuel, parts, and people for the U.S. economy.

You are an experienced operator with deep relationships, sharp commercial instincts, and a credible track record of advancing complex enterprise pursuits inside at least one of these portfolios. You bring fluency in AI, data, SaaS, and enterprise software — enough to hold your own in a technical conversation, and enough to translate operator problems into commercial pathways. You are equally at home in a SCIF, a logistics command operations center, a Capitol Hill office, or a Fortune 100 boardroom.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Mission and Market Development

 - Identify priority mission areas, customer organizations, program offices, operational units, and acquisition pathways where Gallatin can create measurable mission value.

 - Develop account and territory plans across target defense, federal, and commercial customers — including stakeholders, mission owners, technical evaluators, contracting paths, budget signals, partner dependencies, and likely barriers to adoption.

 - Translate operational pain points into clear opportunity narratives that connect customer mission needs to Navigator’s decision support, planning, logistics, sustainment, and workflow capabilities.

 - Track emerging requirements, solicitations, SBIR/STTR opportunities, OTA activity, prototype programs, exercises, experiments, and partner-led pursuits relevant to Gallatin’s growth strategy.

Customer Discovery and Relationship Development

 - Conduct structured customer discovery with operators, planners, logisticians, acquisition personnel, program managers, and technical stakeholders.

 - Develop and maintain relationships with government users, transition partners, system integrators, primes, advisory networks, and ecosystem stakeholders.

 - Prepare for and support c

source_jobs.title: Strategy Operations Associate, Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Gallatin AI is hiring a Strategy & Operations Associate to serve as the principal execution arm for our Head of Growth. This is a rare opportunity for an ambitious, early-career operator to work shoulder-to-shoulder with a senior commercial leader across the full surface area of go-to-market — business development, sales operations and CRM, market and competitive analysis, and partnership development — inside one of the fastest-moving companies in defense tech.

You will stand up and run the operating systems that make the commercial function scale: pipeline instrumentation, account dossiers, competitive intelligence, partnership infrastructure, and the playbooks the rest of the team plugs into. You will be the connective tissue that turns commercial strategy into executed motion — closing the gaps the Head of Growth doesn’t have time to close, and freeing executive bandwidth for the largest strategic and customer-facing moves. You will lean heavily on modern AI tooling (Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT, and emerging agentic systems) to do the work of a much larger team.

This role is built for someone who wants the responsibility curve of an early-stage operator: ambiguous problems, high autonomy, real customers, and a direct line into how a defense AI company gets to scale. The right candidate sees this as a launchpad — not a back-office support function.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Business Development

 - Support the Head of Growth in identifying, qualifying, and advancing opportunities across DoW, allied government, and enterprise defense customers.

 - Build account dossiers, capture briefs, and customer-specific value narratives that arm the commercial team for high-stakes conversations.

 - Drive follow-through on commercial commitments so no thread, request, or follow-up falls through the cracks between meetings.

 - Represent Gallatin in early customer conversations, conferences, and partner forums as the role matures.

Sales Operations & CRM

 - Own the day-to-day health of the CRM — pipeline hygiene, opportunity stages, contact data, and activity instrumentation.

 - Build dashboards, forecasting views, and pipeline reports that give the Head of Growth and senior leadership a clear, real-time view of commercial performance.

 - Design and continuously improve sales processes, playbooks, and workflows as the commercial team scales.

 - Stand up and maintain the tooling stack (CRM, enrichment, intelligence, outbound) and own the integrations between systems.

Market & Competitive Analysis

 - Conduct structured market analysis across defense technology, AI, and logistics segments — sizing opportunities, mapping competitors, and surfacing the signals that should shape Gallatin’s commercial posture.

 - Track program-of-record activity, budget cycles, RFIs/RFPs, and primes / non-traditionals relevant to Gallatin’s roadmap.

 - Synthesize findings into crisp written briefs and decision-grade memos for the Head of Growth, CEO, and Board.

Partnership Development

 - Help build and operate Gallatin’s partner ecosystem — primes, systems integrators, technology partners, and channel relationships across defense and adjacent markets.

 - Manage the partnership pipeline end-to-end: sourcing, structuring, contracting support, joint pursuits, and ongoing partner enablement.

 - Coordinate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, and Legal to translate partnership intent into executed agreements and joint motion.

AI-Native Operating Style

 - Treat modern AI tools (Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT, and emerging agentic systems) as a default extension of your workflow — from

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Marketing Communications
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Manager, Marketing Communications to own how Gallatin shows up in the press and in front of the government decision-makers who matter most to our mission. This is a hands-on, execution-led role for someone who wants to run a public relations program directly, not manage one from a distance.

You will be the person who turns Gallatin’s milestones into coverage, our technical breakthroughs into stories the defense community understands, and our leaders into credible voices in the national security and AI conversation. You will work across the company, with Product, BD, and leadership, to keep the external narrative sharp, accurate, and consistent. The playbook here is still being written, and you will write a lot of it.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Run Public Relations and Media

 - Own Gallatin’s day-to-day press strategy and execution, from pitching and placing coverage to managing inbound media interest.

 - Build and maintain relationships with defense-tech, national security, and AI press and analysts.

 - Run the announcement editorial calendar for product milestones, partnerships, contract awards, and funding, owning timing, message alignment, and amplification.

 - Draft press releases, briefings, talking points, and bylines, and prepare leaders for interviews, panels, and speaking engagements.

Execute Marketing Communications and Content

 - Translate complex, AI-driven capability into clear, value-driven messaging for government decision-makers and non-technical audiences.

 - Produce and maintain core collateral, case studies, web copy, and campaign content in partnership with Product, BD, and Engineering.

 - Keep messaging consistent across channels so what the market reads, hears, and sees all reinforces the same story.

Translate Mission and Capability

 - Serve as a connective layer between Gallatin’s technical work and the narratives that bring it to life for press and DoW audiences.

 - Support executive and internal communications as needed, including all-hands content and milestone messaging.

 - Track what’s working with simple, honest measurement: coverage, share of voice, and engagement tied to the company’s goals.



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 6+ years of progressive communications experience, with a meaningful stretch owning external communications and media relations directly.

 - Exceptional writing and editing across formats, from press releases and bylines to executive talking points and briefings.

 - A proven track record running PR and media programs: pitching, placing coverage, and building durable relationships with reporters and analysts.

 - Demonstrated ability to translate complex or technical subject matter into clear narratives for non-technical and decision-maker audiences.

 - Comfort operating with autonomy, managing announcement cadence end to end, and moving fast without a fully built function around you.

 - A collaborative, low-ego, builder mentality in environments where the playbook is still being written.




BONUS POINTS

 - Experience in defense, government, national security, deep tech, or AI, where translating complexity and earning credibility with technical and government audiences is central.

 - Existing relationships with defense-tech, national security, or AI press and analysts.

 - Experience at a Series A to C stage company, especially through an inflection such as a launch, contract award, or fundraise.

 - Familiarity with DoW program, acquisition, Pentagon, and/or military audiences.



MISSION AND IDENTITY

We are building the system that enables faster, smarter logistics

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding defense logistics for the warfighters of the United States and allied forces. We take an AI-first approach to improve defense readiness through software products that streamline and modernize logistics operations from factory to foxhole and result in better decisions and outcomes.

We believe that you won't change the world by phoning it in and as such we work in-office in one of our core engineering locations: El Segundo, CA; Austin, TX, Washington DC, San Francisco, CA




ABOUT THE ROLE

The Infrastructure team owns the platform that delivers Navigator to the warfighters who depend on it. Our charter spans Kubernetes clusters in commercial and government cloud, application streaming for thin-client users on classified networks, a unified artifact pipeline that signs and ships the same builds to every environment we operate, and air-gapped appliances at forward sites where Contested Logistics is a daily reality, not a slide.

We're hiring an Infrastructure Engineer who builds security into the systems they operate rather than treating it as a separate workstream. You'll architect, build, and run the platform end to end — writing RFCs and PRDs, reviewing and writing production code alongside Product and Engineering teammates, and owning the outcome when it ships. The work cuts across cloud-native deployments, edge appliances, data platforms, and the application streaming layer that puts Navigator on classified environments. You'll partner with our Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) leads and mission owners on STIGs, CVE remediation, audit prep, and the controls work that lets us operate in environments such as IL5, IL6, and JWICS.

Success means a platform that stays up under real operational load, ships trusted artifacts to every profile we run, and passes audits without fire drills.

This position requires the ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance.




WHAT YOU'LL DO




BUILD AND OPERATE THE PLATFORM

 - Design, develop, test, iterate, and deploy secure production systems across cloud-native and edge appliance deployments

 - Own end-to-end infrastructure outcomes for one or more critical programs or priority infrastructure initiatives

 - Harden the artifact pipeline so the same signed builds run in commercial cloud, single-tenant high-side, and air-gapped appliance deployments

 - Run production Kubernetes across commercial and government cloud — operators, networking, storage, and multi-cluster operations that hold up under operational load

 - Keep the platform reliable through proactive monitoring, logging, and alerting, and own the response when something breaks


SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE AS A DAILY DISCIPLINE

 - Embed application and infrastructure security into the systems you build — identity, network segmentation, secrets management, and CVE remediation as part of the work, not a phase at the end

 - Partner with GRC and mission owners on STIG implementation, audit prep, and the controls work that keeps us operating in IL5, IL6, and JWICS environments

 - Maintain an audit-ready posture between formal assessment cycles, so authorization is a steady state rather than a fire drill


COLLABORATE ACROSS DISCIPLINES

 - Embed with cross-functional teams and advise on infrastructure, security, and deployment best practices that hold up in production

 - Work closely with Product and Engineering on RFCs, PRDs, and production code — you contribute to the systems you operate

 - Contribute to engineering best practices: code reviews, documentation, and continuously improving CI/CD and DevSecOps workflows while keeping Developer Experience at the forefront




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

People request you by name because you bias toward action. You're a problem-solver by nature who genuinely cares about the mission and the outcome. You operate without hand-holding — you read a problem, weigh the tradeoffs, and move. Sometimes those tradeoffs have harsh consequen

source_jobs.title: Recruiting Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GALLATIN

At Gallatin, we are rebuilding logistics infrastructure for the national security missions of the United States and allied partners. We build AI systems that determine how logistics decisions are made — not just how they're executed. From factory to foxhole, we operate at the layer where data becomes decisions, and decisions make the advantage.




 


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Recruiting Coordinator to help scale Gallatin during a critical stage of growth. This is a highly operational, high-ownership role focused on delivering an exceptional candidate experience while keeping recruiting running efficiently behind the scenes. You'll own interview coordination, onsite execution, recruiting operations, and the countless details that allow recruiters and hiring managers to move quickly.

This role is based full-time in our El Segundo office and will play a critical role in facilitating onsite interviews and supporting candidates throughout the interview process. You should thrive in fast-moving environments, stay calm under pressure, communicate clearly, and genuinely enjoy being the person who keeps everything organized and moving forward. You're resourceful, proactive, and willing to jump in wherever needed.

This role is perfect for someone interested in building a long-term career in recruiting, recruiting operations, or talent acquisition.




 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Coordinate and schedule virtual interviews across multiple teams and time zones, ensuring a seamless, high-touch candidate experience from scheduling through offer.

 - Facilitate in-person interviews in our El Segundo office, including candidate hosting, interview logistics, room coordination, travel, and day-of execution.

 - Partner closely with recruiters and hiring managers to keep recruiting processes moving quickly and efficiently while serving as the primary point of contact for candidates throughout the interview process.

 - Support recruiting operations across Ashby and other systems by maintaining accurate data, documentation, reporting, and recruiting resources.

 - Identify opportunities to improve recruiting processes, making them more scalable, organized, and candidate-friendly.

 - Thrive in a fast-paced environment by managing multiple competing priorities with urgency, accuracy, and exceptional attention to detail.



 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience in recruiting coordination, recruiting operations, HR coordination, executive scheduling, or another highly operational role.

 - Exceptional organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail.

 - Outstanding written and verbal communication skills.

 - Ability to manage a high volume of scheduling and coordination without sacrificing candidate experience.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment where priorities change quickly.

 - Strong judgment, professionalism, and discretion when working with candidates and executives.

 - Experience working with applicant tracking systems, ideally Ashby.

 - Comfortable learning new technology and using AI to improve efficiency and streamline administrative work.

 - Strong sense of ownership and willingness to jump into whatever is needed to support the recruiting team.

 


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience supporting recruiting at a high-growth startup.

 - Experience coordinating executive or technical hiring processes.

 - Familiarity with Ashby.




Kindly note, this role is five days per week in-person at our El Segundo, CA office.

 


MISSION AND IDENTITY

We are building the system that enables faster, smarter logistics decisions in contested environments, and we're doing it with a team of seasoned entrepreneurs, operators, and technologists who have built and scaled solutions in this space before. We hold ourselves to an extremely high standard. We value clear thinking, direct communication, and the kind of ownership that doesn't stop until something actually works.

Our mission is to create d

notes: Highly technical roles, strong focus on AI and logistics. Multiple locations, but no explicit remote work options.
